title: Getting started
text: figure provides overview R functions R6 classes OSP Global Sensitivity package user access needed run sensitivity analysis workflow. workflow can decomposed three steps: path PKML simulation file , performing sensitivity analyses drug-drug interaction (DDI) ratios, path PKML DDI simulation file, via loadSimulation() function. R list model parameters analyzed. parameters provided form objects R6 SAParameter class. instantiation parameter object class, probability distribution provided form R6 object either UniformDistribution, LogUniformDistribution, NormalDistribution, LogNormalDistribution class. R list model outputs analyzed SAOutput objects. object includes path model output PKML simulations well PK parameters evaluated model outputs. runSU() conducts one---time local sensitivity uncertainty analyses. runMorris() conducts Morris screening. runSobol() runEFAST() conduct variance-based sensitivity analyses using Sobol EFAST algorithms respectively. generateTornadoPlot() used plot results one---time local sensitivity uncertainty analysis returned runSU(). generateMorrisPlot() used plot results Morris screening returned runMorris(). generateSobolBarGraph() generateEFASTBarGraph() plot results variance-based sensitivity analyses returned runSobol() runEFAST() respectively. Overview R functions R6 classes OSP Global Sensitivity package.

title: Getting started
text: model required PKML simulation format may generated PK-Sim MoBi simulation. user enters full path file load using loadSimulation() function:
code: simFilePath <- "path/to/simulation-file.pkml" simulation <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # A simulation with a DDI can similarly be loaded if sensitivity analysis of # DDI ratios is required: DDIsimFilePath <- "path/to/DDI-simulation-file.pkml" DDIsimulation <- loadSimulation(DDIsimFilePath)

title: Getting started
text: convenient way access paths parameters outputs simulation use getSimulationTree() function ospsuite package: user next provides R list model parameters SAParameter objects. object includes path every input parameter PKML simulations well probability distribution input parameter. inputs specification input parameter : simulation (required): simulation object previously loaded, parameter must located. DDIsimulation (optional): previously loaded DDI simulation object, parameter must located exact path simulation object. path (required): path input parameter PKML simulation objects simulation DDIsimulation (provided). displayName (optional): convenient short name input parameter can used plots tables. parameterDistribution (optional): probability distribution object input parameter, selected among four R6 classes: UniformDistribution, LogUniformDistribution, NormalDistribution, LogNormalDistribution. unit (optional): non-dimensionless parameters, input allows user specify units descriptors probability distribution parameter, mean variance normal distribution. provided, units probability distribution assumed base units dimension parameter PKML simulation. list input parameters can created follows: , four input parameters defined. first path within simulation encoded output tree$path$$parameter1$path. dimension Length LogNormal distribution best described units micrometers (µm) mean 10 CV (coefficient variation) 0.5. unit (µm) can easily specified using ospUnits list provided ospsuite. contrast, second parameter Uniform distribution. second parameter dimensionless, therefore units distribution specified. Note descriptors two distributions different. Uniform LogUniform distributions take parameters minimum maximum. Normal distribution takes mean standard deviation (stdv) input. distribution specified parameter, parameter3 parameter4, default LogUniform distribution used ranges within factor 10% nominal value parameter simulation object. Note default value input parameter zero, upward downward scaling parameter factor 10% yield variation.
code: tree <- getSimulationTree(simulation) parametersList <- list(   SAParameter$new(simulation = simulation,                   path = tree$path$to$parameter1$path,                   displayName = "parameter1",                   unit = ospUnits$Length$µm,                   parameterDistribution = LogNormalDistribution$new(mean = 10, CV = 0.5)   ),   SAParameter$new(simulation = simulation,                   path = tree$path$to$parameter2$path,                   displayName = "parameter2",                   parameterDistribution = UniformDistribution$new(minimum = 0, maximum = 1)   ),   SAParameter$new(simulation = simulation,                   path = tree$path$to$parameter3$path,                   displayName = "parameter3"   ),   SAParameter$new(simulation = simulation,                   path = tree$path$to$parameter4$path,                   displayName = "parameter4"   ) )

title: Getting started
text: specify model quantity sensitivity parameters evaluated, user inputs : path (required): path model output PKML simulation object, must also exist DDIsimulation object provided, displayName (optional): convenient short name model output can used plots tables, PK parameters evaluated model outputs (required). Two model outputs, output1 output2, PK parameters, added following example: , sensitivity PK parameters C_max AUC_tEnd output output1 evaluated respect parameters defined parametersList . output output2, PK parameter C_max evaluated. Note AUC_tEnd denotes AUC output end simulation time, set simulation MoBi PK-Sim. list available PK parameters may printed using ospsuite command allPKParameterNames(). also possible evaluate PK parameters output specific time period simulation, sensitivity particular interest. example, user may examine model input parameters impact AUC drug’s concentration time profile early phase drug administered. case, time period PK parameter evaluated set 0 100 minutes follows:
code: output1 <- SAOutput$new(simulation = simulation,                         DDIsimulation = DDIsimulation,                         path = tree$path$to$output1$path,                         displayName = "output1") output1$addPKParameter(standardPKParameter = "C_max") output1$addPKParameter(standardPKParameter = "AUC_tEnd")  output2 <- SAOutput$new(simulation = simulation,                         DDIsimulation = DDIsimulation,                         path = tree$path$to$output2$path,                         displayName = "output2") output2$addPKParameter(standardPKParameter = "C_max") outputList <- list(output1, output2) output1$addPKParameter(standardPKParameter = "AUC_tEnd",                        pkParameterDisplayName = "AUC_early",                        startTime = 0,                        endTime = 100)

what: Running one-at-a-time analyses (local and uncertainty analysis)
title: Getting started
text: local uncertainty analyses may run together. following example, runSU() function used compute local sensitivity uncertainty analysis outputs defined outputList respect parameters defined parametersList. : simulation argument takes simulation object, optional DDIsimulation argument takes DDI simulation object, customParameters argument optionally takes parametersList input. argument provided evaluateForAllParameters FALSE, local sensitivity analysis evaluated parameters list, using probability distributions set parameters. customParameters provided evaluateForAllParameters TRUE, analyses performed constant parameters model, assuming LogUniform distribution scales parameter upward downward variationRange respect nominal value simulation. outputs argument takes outputList list, defines output paths PK parameters local sensitivity uncertainty analyzed. input evaluateForAllParameters set TRUE, local sensitivity uncertainty analyses evaluated constant parameters model. argument variationRange sets fraction parameters perturbed local sensitivity analysis, default value 0.1. argument numberOfSensitivityAnalysisSteps sets number steps within variationRange evaluate local sensitivity analysis. argument sensitivityThreshold, default value 0.1, can used set local sensitivity value minimum allowable parameter included among sensitivity results. argument can used exclude parameters little impact model outputs perturbed. runUncertaintyAnalysis set FALSE, local sensitivity analysis evaluated. runUncertaintlyOnlyForSensitiveParameters set TRUE, uncertainty analysis evaluated parameters local sensitivity sensitivityThreshold. quantiles input used set percentiles PK parameters evaluated addition 50% 95% percentiles, computed default. argument must vector numbers 0 1. numberOfUncertaintyAnalysisSamples argument used set number Monte Carlo samples drawn parameter distributions PK parameters evaluated uncertainty analysis. saveFolder saveFileName arguments set folder name Excel .xlsx file local sensitivity uncertainty analysis results saved saveResults set TRUE. function generateTornadoPlot() may subsequently used generate tornado plot local sensitivity uncertainty analysis results separately, follows:
code: su <- runSU(simulation = simulation,             DDIsimulation = DDIsimulation,             customParameters = parametersList,             outputs = outputList,             evaluateForAllParameters = FALSE,             # Sensitivity analysis parameters:             variationRange = 0.2,             numberOfSensitivityAnalysisSteps = 2,             sensitivityThreshold = 0.1,             # Uncertainty analysis parameters:             runUncertaintyAnalysis = TRUE,             runUncertaintlyOnlyForSensitiveParameters = TRUE,             quantiles = c(0.25, 0.75),             numberOfUncertaintyAnalysisSamples = 10,             saveResults = TRUE,             saveFileName = "sensitivityUncertaintyResults.xlsx",             saveFolder = "path/to/folder/where/results/are/saved/") # Generate a `ggplot` tornado plot for local sensitivity analysis: plt <- generateTornadoPlot(sensitivityDataFrame = su$Results,                            generateForUncertaintyAnalysis = FALSE)  # Generate a `ggplot` tornado plot for uncertainty analysis: plt <- generateTornadoPlot(sensitivityDataFrame = su$Results,                            generateForUncertaintyAnalysis = TRUE)

title: Getting started
text: run Morris algorithm 100 trajectories previously defined simulation, DDIsimulation, parametersList outputList, runMorris() function used. function generateMorrisPlot() may subsequently used generate Morris plot:
code: # Run Morris sensitivity analysis: morrisResults <- runMorris(simulation = simulation,                            DDIsimulation = DDIsimulation,                            parameters = parametersList,                            outputs = outputList,                            numberOfSamples = 100)  # Generate a `ggplot` Morris plot for the results: plt <- generateMorrisPlot(morrisResults$Results)

what: Running variance-based Global Sensitivity Analyses (Sobol and EFAST)
title: Getting started
text: run variance-based Sobol EFAST global sensitivity analysis methods, functions runSobol() runEFAST() used. generateSobolBarGraph() generateEFASTBarGraph() functions may used generate ggplot bar graphs results two methods:
code: # Run Sobol sensitivity analysis: sobolResults <- runSobol(simulation = simulation,                          DDIsimulation = DDIsimulation,                          parameters = parametersList,                          outputs = outputList,                          numberOfSamples = 1000)  # Run EFAST sensitivity analysis: EFASTresults <- runEFAST(simulation = simulation,                          DDIsimulation = DDIsimulation,                          parameters = parametersList,                          outputs = outputList,                          numberOfResamples = 1)  # Generate a `ggplot` bar graph of Sobol sensitivity analysis results: pltSobol <- generateSobolBarGraph(sobolResults$Results)  # Generate a `ggplot` bar graph of EFAST sensitivity analysis results: pltEFAST <- generateEFASTBarGraph(EFASTresults$Results)

what: Using the graphical user interface and R script generation
title: Getting started
text: R Shiny graphical user interface (GUI) provided OSP Global Sensitivity package facilitate setup execution sensitivity algorithms. app, consists four tabs, launched first loading package using library(ospsuite.globalsensitivity) command, followed runGUI() command: PKML simulation file, well optional DDI simulation file, may uploaded via Start tab application. Parameters tab, tree structure simulation paths generated uploaded PKML. tree enables user select model input parameters analyzed. parameters selected tree, clicking Specify distributions button creates miniature forms user may select probability distribution parameter. Outputs tab, user similarly selects output quantities analyzed tree structure generated PKML simulation. Clicking Specify PK parameters button generates miniature form user can select PK parameters analyzed model output. final Run sensitivity analyses tab, user selects sensitivity algorithm run specifies run settings. run, progress bar appears bottom right application. Upon completion, user may download sensitivity result form Excel .xlsx files, download visualizations sensitivity results. addition, clicking Get code button corresponding sensitivity algorithms generates R script analyses may run R. R script generated based user-specified input parameters distributions well user-selected outputs PK parameters. four tabs accompanying R Shiny app graphical user interface OSP Global Sensitivity package.

title: Midazolam DDI example
text: Two PK-Sim models, previously developed Hanke et al. (2018), represent midazolam midazolam-plus-rifampicin dosing scenarios reported Backman et al. control trial, 15 mg oral midazolam tablet administered standalone treatment, whereas test arm tablet given 17 hours following conclusion 5-day regimen involving daily dose 600 mg rifampicin. two models, may downloaded Rifampicin-Midazolam-DDI repository, simulate midazolam plasma concentration two dosing scenarios European adult male.

title: Midazolam DDI example
text: Six parameters analyzed, spanning metabolism midazolam, distribution, induction CYP3A4 rifampicin:

title: Midazolam DDI example
text: example begins loading OSP Global Sensitivity package followed midazolam simulation midazolam/rifampicin DDI simulation:
code: rm(list = ls()) # Load the OSP Global Sensitivity R package library(ospsuite.globalsensitivity)  # Load Midazolam model simulation including Rifampicin simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "DDI Control - Midazolam - Backman 1996 - with Rif.pkml",                            package = "ospsuite.globalsensitivity") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # Load Midazolam model simulation including DDI with Rifampicin DDIsimFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "DDI Treatment - Rifampicin_Midazolam - Backman 1996.pkml",                               package = "ospsuite.globalsensitivity") DDIsim <- loadSimulation(DDIsimFilePath)  # Generate a list containing paths of all parameters in the model for # convenience of parameter lookup tree <- getSimulationTree(sim)

text: Next, model output PK parameters output, sensitivity analyzed, defined. midazolam plasma concentration peripheral venous blood analyzed, C_max AUC_inf PK parameters interest:

title: Midazolam DDI example
text: Finally, sensitivity analyses run. Providing DDIsimulation argument causes package evaluate sensitivity ratios PK parameters treatment (DDIsim) control (sim) simulations:
code: # Run local sensitivity analysis and uncertainty analysis su <- runSU(simulation = sim,             DDIsimulation = DDIsim,             customParameters = parametersList,             outputs = outputList,             evaluateForAllParameters = FALSE,             # Sensitivity analysis parameters:             variationRange = 0.2,             numberOfSensitivityAnalysisSteps = 2,             sensitivityThreshold = 0,             # Uncertainty analysis parameters:             runUncertaintyAnalysis = TRUE,             numberOfUncertaintyAnalysisSamples = 100,             quantiles = c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95))  # Run Morris sensitivity analysis morrisResults <- runMorris(simulation = sim,                            DDIsimulation = DDIsim,                            parameters = parametersList,                            outputs = outputList,                            numberOfSamples = 100)  # Run Sobol sensitivity analysis sobolResults <- runSobol(simulation = sim,                          DDIsimulation = DDIsim,                          parameters = parametersList,                          outputs = outputList,                          numberOfSamples = 100)  # Run EFAST sensitivity analysis efastResults <- runEFAST(simulation = sim,                          DDIsimulation = DDIsim,                          parameters = parametersList,                          outputs = outputList,                          numberOfResamples = 1)

title: UV filter formulation example
text: run example, load OSP Global Sensitivity package UV filter simulation either octocrylene oxybenzone:
code: rm(list = ls()) # Load the OSP Global Sensitivity R package library(ospsuite.globalsensitivity)  # Load octocrylene model simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "octocrylene-gsa.pkml", package = "ospsuite.globalsensitivity") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)  # OR  # Load oxybenzone model simulation simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "oxybenzone-gsa.pkml", package = "ospsuite.globalsensitivity") sim <- loadSimulation(simFilePath)

title: UV filter formulation example
text: Specify input parameters sensitivity evaluated:
code: # Create a list of Parameter objects corresponding to parameters that exist in # the octocrylene model. parametersList <- list(   SAParameter$new(simulation = sim,                   path = "DERMAL_APPLICATION_AREA|vehicle|scale_factor_vehicle_evaporation",                   displayName = "scale_factor_vehicle_evaporation",                   unit = "",                   parameterDistribution = LogUniformDistribution$new(minimum = 1e-2, maximum = 1e2)   ),   SAParameter$new(simulation = sim,                   path = "DERMAL_APPLICATION_AREA|vehicle|delta_trans",                   displayName = "delta_trans",                   unit = ospUnits$Dimensionless$Unitless,                   parameterDistribution = LogUniformDistribution$new(minimum = 1e-1, maximum = 1e1)   ),   SAParameter$new(simulation = sim,                   path = "DERMAL_APPLICATION_AREA|vehicle|fraction_vehicle_volume_non_volatile",                   displayName = "fraction_vehicle_volume_non_volatile",                   unit = "",                   parameterDistribution = UniformDistribution$new(minimum = 0.2, maximum = 0.8)   ),   SAParameter$new(simulation = sim,                   path = "DERMAL_APPLICATION_AREA|vehicle|beta",                   displayName = "beta"   ) )

title: UV filter formulation example
text: Define output PK parameter, sensitivity analyzed. whole-body concentration permeant analyzed, AUC end simulation (AUC_tEnd) PK parameter interest:
code: # Create an Output object corresponding to a simulated quantity that exists in # the octocrylene model. Y <- SAOutput$new(simulation = sim,                   path = "DERMAL_APPLICATION_AREA|in_vivo_sink|permeant|whole_body_concentration",                   displayName = "whole_body_concentration") Y$addPKParameter(standardPKParameter = "AUC_tEnd") outputList <- list(Y)

title: UV filter formulation example
text: Run one---time (local uncertainty) analyses followed Morris, Sobol, EFAST global sensitivity analyses: running script, specify valid saveFolder Excel result files written.
code: # Run local sensitivity analysis and uncertainty analysis su <- runSU(simulation = sim,             customParameters = parametersList,             outputs = outputList,             evaluateForAllParameters = FALSE,             # Sensitivity analysis parameters:             variationRange = 0.2,             numberOfSensitivityAnalysisSteps = 2,             sensitivityThreshold = 0,             # Uncertainty analysis parameters:             runUncertaintyAnalysis = TRUE,             numberOfUncertaintyAnalysisSamples = 100,             quantiles = c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.95),             saveResults = TRUE, saveFolder = "folder/path", saveFileName = "SU-UVFilter.xlsx")  # Run Morris sensitivity analysis morrisResults <- runMorris(simulation = sim,                            parameters = parametersList,                            outputs = outputList,                            numberOfSamples = 100,                            saveResults = TRUE, saveFolder = "folder/path", saveFileName = "morris-UVFilter.xlsx")  # Run Sobol sensitivity analysis sobolResults <- runSobol(simulation = sim,                          parameters = parametersList,                          outputs = outputList,                          numberOfSamples = 2000,                          saveResults = TRUE, saveFolder = "folder/path", saveFileName = "sobol-UVFilter.xlsx")  # Run EFAST sensitivity analysis efastResults <- runEFAST(simulation = sim,                          parameters = parametersList,                          outputs = outputList,                          numberOfResamples = 1,                          saveResults = TRUE, saveFolder = "folder/path", saveFileName = "efast-UVFilter.xlsx")
